{"step_by_step_thinking": "The patient is a 32-year-old female with acidophilic owl eye nucleoli in the left supraclavicular lymph node biopsy, which is immunohistochemically compatible with CD15 and CD30. This description matches the Reed-Sternberg cells seen in Hodgkin lymphoma. Among the options, Nodular Sclerosis Hodgkin's lymphoma (A) is the most likely diagnosis as it is characterized by the presence of acidophilic nucleoli and Reed-Sternberg cells. Lymphocytic Predominant Hodgkin's Lymphoma (B) does not typically show Reed-Sternberg cells. Reactive nodular hyperplasia (C) is a reactive process and does not show the specific Reed-Sternberg cells. Large cell lymphoma (D) would not show the characteristic acidophilic nucleoli and Reed-Sternberg cells.", "answer_choice": "A"}
